meta.note: Complete instrument. Gold tags are hidden from the agent and used only by the report engine. Delivery order: routing -> core spine -> pro (+battery core +add-on) -> expert (+battery deep).

goldTagLegend.CF-sink: table-stakes; collected but SUPPRESSED from findings

L1.prompt: Why is this guest staying with you?
L1.grounding: Picture one guest you know who fits — answer as them.

L2.note: Party routing — unchanged from v4.1. C opens Family add-on; D–H open Group add-on.

core.prompt: Some guests almost book, then pick a different place. What is the most common reason?

options.label: No rooms free for their dates
A rule they did not like (deposit, cancellation)
a hotel closer to where they're going
a place with parking or a shuttle
they didn't book elsewhere — just didn't come

core.prompt: Silent complaints they never write in a review.

options.label: how a staff member treated them
slow or no response to a request

core.prompt: Rooms you try NOT to give this guest.
Does this guest eat at your hotel restaurant?

options.label: Only coffee or a quick item
No — eats in the room or off-site
No — eats somewhere else (the ship, the venue, near the hospital)

core.prompt: If they do NOT use the restaurant, where do they eat?
What makes this guest feel staff really care about them?

options.label: Their room was ready and quiet when they arrived
A staff member fixed a problem for them
Staff did something extra without being asked
Nothing — they feel like just another guest

core.prompt: When something goes wrong during the stay, what does this guest usually do?

options.label: Says nothing, and never comes back
Says nothing, but tells friends or co-workers
Tells staff, and gives another chance if it is fixed well
Complains, and never comes back anyway

core.prompt: A small free thing you've seen make this guest very happy.

options.label: a small free item (coffee, water, snack)
early coffee before the café opens
a quiet, ready room without asking

core.prompt: What they ask for too late, when you can no longer do it well.
What they ask the front desk for that the hotel does NOT have.

options.label: help finding something nearby (pharmacy, cash, late food)
something to do at night when nothing's open
early coffee before the café opens
a ride at an odd hour
a quiet place to work or wait
go out and get it themselves
ask you where to find it

core.prompt: What this guest needs that most other guests do NOT.

pro.prompt: What is the MAIN reason this guest books your hotel?

options.label: Close to where they need to be (walking distance)
The hotel brand or loyalty points
They stayed before — it is a habit

pro.prompt: For this guest, mark each: must-have / nice extra / does not matter.
What do you do for this guest that is NOT an official rule, but always works?
Which hotel rule do you most often say sorry to this guest for?
What do these guests say another hotel does, that yours does not?
What do you do again and again for this guest that a small change could stop?
Before they arrive, what do these guests call or email to ask about?
How does this guest usually arrive?

options.label: drives, and leaves the car here during a cruise
flies in, then takes a taxi or rideshare
comes by transit or on foot
drives on a busy event night and struggles to park

pro.prompt: What would this guest happily pay extra for?
Do you serve this guest type more often than the hotel is set up for?
If yes, what is missing for them?
If you could change ONE thing for this guest for free, what would it be?

expert.prompt: What moment makes this guest decide if they will come back?

options.label: How they were welcomed at arrival
How they were treated at check-out
A certain thing that happens every stay

expert.prompt: A guest complains about one thing, but the real problem is something else. Give one example.
What does this guest often do that surprises new staff?
Some guests seem ordinary but are actually important. Have you seen this with these guests?
How can staff spot this guest early?

items.prompt: For a business guest, mark each: must-have / nice extra / does not matter.

options.label: a good place to work in the room

items.prompt: Does this business guest eat dinner at your restaurant?
One thing that would save them time.

options.label: a ready, quiet room without asking
luggage hold for a late flight

items.prompt: Most-forgotten items (the sundries market).
Mark each: must-have / nice extra / does not matter.

options.label: a dark, quiet room for a short sleep
a wake-up call and ride to the airport

items.prompt: How early does this guest leave?
Late/early need when nothing's open.

options.label: hot food on a late arrival
a quiet room away from 5am noise

items.prompt: Mark each: must-have / nice extra / does not matter.

options.label: a nice view or special touch

items.prompt: Does this holiday guest use and enjoy your restaurant?
Wish they'd known / packed for Seattle.
What does a cruise guest need most? Pick up to 3.

options.label: leave the car here during the cruise
leave very early for the port
a quick shop or grocery run

items.prompt: Does this cruise guest use your restaurant?
Items they ask the desk for.
Why a cruise guest picks a cruise-friendlier hotel.
What does an event guest need most? Pick up to 3.

options.label: late check-out after a late night

items.prompt: Does this event guest use your restaurant?
What you do differently on a busy event night.

options.label: a quiet room from the crowd

items.prompt: What does a medical guest need most? Pick up to 3.

options.label: close to the hospital, or a shuttle

items.prompt: What food does this guest need?
Medical item or errand they need.
Mark each: must-have / nice extra / does not matter.

options.label: a dark quiet room for daytime sleep

items.prompt: When do they mostly sleep?
What crew need that you're not set up for. Pick all you've seen.

options.label: a quiet floor from day noise

items.prompt: Is crew a real, recurring guest type for you?
items.note: segment-existence check — gates ALL crew findings (GoldMap §14)

items.prompt: Mark each: must-have / nice extra / does not matter.
What do they value MORE?
What they need that you're not set up for. Pick all you've seen.

options.label: staff briefed NOT to make a fuss
to NOT be upgraded loudly / announced

items.prompt: When you give the full VIP treatment (amenity, greeting, announced upgrade), how do they react?

options.label: They prefer to be left alone

items.prompt: How do families eat at your hotel?
What does a family need most in their room? Pick up to 2.

options.label: Space for a cot or crib
A fridge for kids' food and milk
A room near the pool (or far from it)

items.prompt: Harder for a family than a couple.
Kids' item you don't have.
A small thing you've seen make a parent's stay much easier.
Who you must keep happy.
What goes wrong with groups.
Problem a group causes other guests.

options.label: taking over the lobby / common areas

items.prompt: What makes a group organiser book with you again?
